# Camera Roll PII Redaction Cost Estimates \- After M2

Author:  [Owen Lee](mailto:owen.lee@match.com)  
Last updated:  Feb 3, 2026

# Assumptions

- During the Camera Roll Insights experiment, monthly infrastructure costs may fluctuate based on the actual opt-in rate.  
- Assume a single user interacts with the Camera Roll scan module **once per month**  
  - i.e., no rescans within a month.  
  - Conversely, if a user uses the Camera Roll feature in March and then doesn't use it again in April, the number of users may decrease.  
- The number of photos per user is decreased to **100 from 600**. Photo resolution has been changed to 256x256 from 224x224.

# Cost Projection by Scenario

In M1, minimum cluster (4 CPU \+ 8 GPU pods) handles 140 TPM with excellent latency (P99 2.5s). From M2, we expect to handle **700** TPM with a minimum cluster with the change of the number of photos per user and photo resolution.

| Target Region | Exp. User Base (daily) | Exp.Throughput (TPM) | Est. Daily Cost (before discount) | Est. Daily Cost (after 21% discount) | Configuration Detail |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Baseline (Min)** | 0 \- 950K | **700 TPM** | $264 | **$209** | 4 CPU(`m7g.4xlarge`) \+8 GPU(`g5.2xlarge`) Pods |
| **AUS 100%, feature adoption rate 50%** | 380K | **264 TPM** | $264 | **$209** | Same with baseline.No scale out required. |
| **USA 100%, feature adoption rate 50%** | 1.3M | **900 TPM** | $396 | **$313** | Scale out Baseline x \~1.5 |
| **AUS 100% \+ CAN 100% \+ USA 30%** \* **,feature adoption rate 50% \*\*** *2026 market expansion plan* | 795K(190K \+ 215K \+ 390K) | **550 TPM** | $264 | **$209** | Same with baseline.No scale out required. |
| **AUS 100% \+ CAN 100% \+ USA 100%** \*,**feature adoption rate 50%** | 1.7M(190K \+ 215K \+ 1.3M) | **1,200 TPM** | $528 | **$418** | Scale out Baseline x \~2 |
| **Global 100%, feature adoption rate 50%** | 20M | **14,000 TPM** | $5,280 | **$4,180** | Scale out Baseline x \~20 |

TPM (Transactions Per Minute) requirements are derived to process total daily traffic within 24 hours.

\* Testing markets and expected opt-in users

- AUS, **50%** eligible users (100% rollout, 50% feature opt-in ratio)  
- CAN, **50%** eligible users (100% rollout, 50% feature opt-in ratio)  
- USA, **15%** eligible users (30% A/B test exposure, 50% feature opt-in ratio)

# Details: Unit Metrics & Baseline Capacity (Resource Basis)

The system consists of three distinct microservices. To ensure **High Availability (HA)** across 4 Availability Zones (AZs), the **minimum operational baseline is 1 Pod per AZ (Total 4 Pods)** for each service.

| Service Name | Role | Resource Type | Min. Configuration | Unit Cost per Pod |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **camerarollpiiredaction** | Orchestration (FastAPI) | **CPU** (`m7g.4xlarge`) | 4 Pods (2 Pods/Node) | $0.326/hr |
| **mlyoloface** | ML Model (Seldon-core) | **GPU** (`g5.2xlarge`) | 4 Pods (1 Pod/Node) | $1.212/hr |
| **mltextdetection** | ML Model (Seldon-core) | **GPU** (`g5.2xlarge`) | 4 Pods (1 Pod/Node) | $1.212/hr |

The current minimum cluster (4 CPU \+ 8 GPU pods) handles **140 TPM** with excellent latency (P99 2.5s). As demonstrated in the Locust benchmark below, the system maintains a stable P99 latency of \~2.5s under a concurrency of 30\.

![][image1]

####
